config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang kuat agar “kepentingan masyarakat” tidak dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau berbeda pendapat. Saya paham masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan solidaritas, tetapi kalau hak pribadi selalu dikorbankan, negara mudah kebablasan dan kebebasan warga bisa hilang pelan-pelan. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From my American perspective, I am wary of any vague appeal to "society" because it can be used to justify government overreach, punish unpopular minorities, or silence dissent in the name of the common good. At the same time, rights are not a license to ignore harm to others, so public safety and shared rules do matter, but only when they are narrowly justified and do not erase basic liberties. In practice, I would put individual rights first and let society override them only in limited, clearly defined cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya lebih menguntungkan penguasa atau mayoritas, sementara kelompok kecil justru yang paling mudah dirugikan. Saya setuju masyarakat perlu tertib dan saling menjaga, tetapi itu tidak boleh berarti hak dasar orang bisa disisihkan begitu saja. Yang lebih masuk akal adalah menempatkan hak individu sebagai batas utama, lalu kepentingan umum hanya boleh didahulukan dalam keadaan yang benar-benar jelas, perlu, dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am especially suspicious of broad claims about the "common good" because they are often used to justify censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular people. Public safety and social order do matter, but they should be limited by clear constitutional rights and only override them in narrow, well-justified situations. If society always comes first, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa sering dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk menekan minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau melindungi kepentingan yang lebih kuat, jadi saya tidak percaya pada klaim itu kalau tidak dibatasi dengan sangat ketat. Memang ketertiban umum dan keselamatan bersama penting, tetapi itu hanya sah mengalahkan hak individu dalam keadaan yang benar-benar jelas, perlu, dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, yang paling sering dikorbankan bukan “individu egois,” melainkan warga biasa yang paling lemah suaranya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I do not trust broad appeals to the "common good" because they are often a cover for censorship, discrimination, or simple government overreach, and the people who get hurt first are usually minorities or dissenters. Public safety and shared rules are real, but they only deserve to override rights in narrow, clearly justified, and proportional cases. If we flip the default and put society first, the result is usually that the powerful decide what counts as society and everyone else pays the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
